text: Present: Daisy Damskey, Paul Connors, Walter Kieser, Bryce Jones, Brian Sullivan, and Hal Hinkle.
President Damskey opened the meeting and brought up the ongoing work that the County has
undertaken to prevent the Russian River bank from further erosion, thus protecting River Road. It was
agreed that we would draft a letter of appreciation to Johannes Hoevertsz, the County Transportation
and Public Works Director. Walter volunteered to prepare a draft that will go on GPC letterhead.
No changes to Agenda were suggested.
Approval of Minutes and Meeting Agenda
Minutes of the February GPC Meetings were reviewed. Minutes were approved, subject to minor
changes suggested by Member Petersen, on a motion by Member Hinkle, seconded by Member Jones.
1. Public Comments on Items Not on Agenda
There was no outside correspondence or communication noted by GPC members.
No items on the consent calendar (the Consent Calendar will include items such as authorizing
routine payments of invoices and approval of contracts, etc., that have been previously discussed).
No financial report was given (Treasurer Peterson is absent).
No Project referrals have recently been received from PRMD and no projects were formally
Member Bryce Jones attended a County Community Development Commission meeting to learn
more about their programs (which center on supporting affordable housing in the County) and how
they might be of assistance in Geyserville, including supporting affordable housing and related
infrastructure funding. There will be subsequent follow-­‐up to determine whether they might have
funding for supporting park construction and other community improvements.
Member Connors reported that the Geyserville Fire Department had set up a committee to help
raise $50,000 for a new “chipper” (a tool that reduced wood waste to mulch). He suggested that
GPC members could demonstrate leadership by making donations and helping with the fund-­‐raising
There was further discussion regarding potential coordination between the GPC and the newly
formed Geyserville/Alexander Valley MAC. It was agreed that the GPC would offer a plan for such
coordination and submit it to Supervisor James Gore in advance of the Community Meeting in April
where he would have the opportunity to present the objectives of the MAC (related to establishing
funding priorities) and how the work previously completed by the GPC (the Community Survey, etc.)
can contribute to the effort going forward.
On an unanimously approved motion it was agreed that Member Kieser would send Supervisor Gore
a quick email notifying him that the GPC would be making a formal proposal for coordination
between the GPC and the Mac following a review of a draft that would be prepared in advance of
the March 24th meeting by Members Hinkle and Kieser.
There was discussion regarding the agenda for the Community meeting, specifically, who would be
invited to have “speaking” roles. While all of the local government representatives would be invited
to participate in the meeting, including the School District, Fire District, Cal Am Water, the County
Sheriff, etc., we want to limit presentations to a few minutes to keep the meeting moving. A draft
agenda for the Community meeting will be prepared for consideration of the Board by President
Damskey. If there are no “big issues” pending the GPC will encourage agencies to submit their
The GPC meeting of March 19, 2019 was adjourned at 5:30 PM. The next meeting of the GPC,
meeting, will be held on Tuesday, March 26, 2019 at 7:00 PM. The meeting is open to the public
who are encouraged to attend and participate.
